decks.notes: This deck focuses on efficiently disrupting the opponent's game plan while maintaining a fast tempo with aggressive creature plays. It combines powerful disruption like Force of Will and Thoughtseize with efficient removal like Fatal Push and tempo plays like Daze to control the board and pressure the opponent. The use of high-value threats like Murktide Regent and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student further adds to the aggressive tempo strategy. Additionally, the deck utilizes powerful lands like Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's mana base and maintain control of the game.

The main idea of this deck is to lock down the opponent's resources by using a combination of artifacts that prevent them from playing their spells effectively. The deck utilizes cards like Trinisphere, Kozilek's Command, and Pithing Needle to disrupt the opponent's game plan. It also includes powerful control elements like Ugin, Karn, and Emrakul to finish off the game once the opponent is locked out. The deck can generate a large amount of mana with cards like Grim Monolith and Urza's lands to power out its threats quickly.

This deck focuses on early disruption and removal to control the opponent's board while quickly deploying threats of its own to close out the game. By leveraging efficient spells like Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, and Daze, it aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and maintain tempo advantage. The deck also utilizes powerful threats like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares, Nethergoyf, and Murktide Regent to pressure the opponent and secure a quick victory. With a mix of disruption, removal, and threats, this deck aims to be a versatile and aggressive force in the Legacy format.

The deck focuses on creating a high storm count to cast powerful spells, primarily using artifacts and rituals to generate mana quickly. It includes a mix of tutors, discard spells, and win conditions like Tendrils of Agony and Ad Nauseam. The goal is to assemble the necessary pieces to either storm off in a single turn or set up a lethal Tendrils of Agony finish. The deck's flexibility, speed, and ability to win out of nowhere make it a formidable combo archetype in the Legacy format.

This Dimir Tempo deck focuses on putting pressure on the opponent while disrupting their game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will. It utilizes efficient creatures like Barrowgoyf and Orcish Bowmasters to quickly close out games, while also controlling the board with removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out. The deck's mana base is tuned to support its game plan, with fetch lands like Polluted Delta and Verdant Catacombs providing consistency. Overall, the deck aims to win through a combination of aggression and disruption, overwhelming the opponent before they can establish their own strategy.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly establish board presence with aggressive creatures like Delver of Secrets and Murktide Regent, supported by disruption spells like Daze and Force of Will. The deck aims to control the game tempo with cards like Wasteland and Stifle while maintaining card advantage through card selection spells like Brainstorm and Ponder. Brazen Borrower provides additional versatility and removal options. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure early on while disrupting opponent's game plan to secure a quick victory in the Legacy format.

The deck focuses on using cards like Show and Tell, Sneak Attack, and Atraxa, Grand Unifier to put powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn into play quickly, overwhelming the opponent with high-powered threats. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect its combo pieces, while also utilizing card selection with Ponder and Brainstorm to find key pieces. Overall, the goal is to cheat out big creatures and win the game before the opponent can react effectively.

The main focus of this deck is to apply constant pressure on the opponent through aggressive plays while disrupting their game plan with countermagic and land destruction. The deck prioritizes efficiency and synergy between low-cost spells and tempo-oriented creatures like Delver of Secrets and Dragon's Rage Channeler. By utilizing cards like Force of Will and Daze to protect its threats and the ability to generate advantage through cards like Mishra's Bauble and Flow State, this deck aims to maintain control of the game through a mix of aggression and disruption.

The main strategy of the deck is to establish early board control with efficient creatures like Badgermole Cub and Birds of Paradise, while also disrupting the opponent's mana base with cards like Wasteland and Gaea's Cradle. The deck aims to ramp up quickly using cards such as Green Sun's Zenith and Natural Order to bring out powerful threats like Craterhoof Behemoth. Additionally, the deck includes a toolbox aspect with cards like Endurance and Fiend Artisan to handle various situations. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of mana acceleration and powerful creatures.

This deck revolves around utilizing lands as resources to control the game. It focuses on using cards like Wasteland, Thespian's Stage, and Life from the Loam to disrupt your opponent's mana base while ramping up your own resources. Crop Rotation and Expedition Map can tutor for specific utility lands like Dark Depths or Boseiju, Who Endures. The goal is to grind out your opponent with powerful land interactions while protecting your own board with cards like Maze of Ith and Karakas. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent through strategic land manipulation and control.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by countering opponent's spells, disrupting their mana base, and drawing cards while setting up win conditions like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. The deck aims to establish control of the game through tempo plays and efficient removal spells such as Swords to Plowshares. It also utilizes powerful cards like Force of Will and Teferi, Time Raveler to maintain board advantage and outvalue opponents. The deck focuses on card advantage, disruption, and efficient threats to outmaneuver and outlast opponents in a grindy game.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures and then bringing them back to the battlefield for a game-winning combo. It uses cards like Unmask and Faithless Looting to discard key creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Griselbrand. By utilizing reanimation spells such as Reanimate and Animate Dead, the deck can bring these creatures back from the graveyard with minimal cost. Cabal Therapy helps to disrupt the opponent's strategy, while cards like Lotus Petal provide mana acceleration to execute the combo efficiently. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with high-powered threats early in the game.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful reanimation spells like Reanimate and Animate Dead to cheat big, game-changing creatures like Archon of Cruelty, Griselbrand, and Koma, World-Eater onto the battlefield quickly. It also includes disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Cabal Therapy to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to efficiently combo off by quickly filling the graveyard and then reanimating a creature to close out the game before the opponent can respond. It also includes some card advantage and control elements with cards like Faithless Looting and Collective Brutality to help sculpt the perfect hand.

The main strategy of this deck is to cheat giant creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play using cards like Show and Tell and Omniscience. The deck also includes cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Stock Up to help with card draw and manipulation, while also including disruption cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to protect the combo. The deck aims to quickly assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures before they have a chance to respond.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize a fast combo strategy that relies on casting a series of spells quickly to win the game before the opponent can respond. By using cards like Agadeem's Awakening, Dark Ritual, and Lotus Petal, the deck can quickly empty its hand and play out all its cards, especially creatures like Balustrade Spy and Boggart Trawler that have powerful effects when put into the graveyard. The goal is to mill through the deck and win with cards like Thassa's Oracle or Pact of Negation.

This deck focuses on controlling the game using a combination of removal spells, counter spells, and card advantage through cards like Uro, Titan of Nature's Wrath and Omen of the Sea. It also utilizes the companion mechanic with Yorion, Sky Nomad for additional value. The deck can generate additional resources through cards like Living Wish and Estrid's Invocation, while also disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Leyline Binding and Dress Down. The goal is to outvalue the opponent and control the game until securing a win condition.
